understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level in sequence, where a level is won only when every goal tile marked '+' is occupied simultaneously by a selectable piece (one piece per goal), and then continue until the run reports WIN for the final level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently controlled piece is identified by player.id and player.position, and movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) attempt to shift that selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. Walls '#' can block or constrain movement in direction-dependent ways, and adjacent pushable objects (blocks 'B') can be pushed if immediately in the movement direction and space allows, causing the block to move along with the selected piece; some boundaries may behave unexpectedly so blocked flags are only hints. If there are multiple selectable pieces, CLICK with an exact pixel-coordinate target_position matching another selectable switches control to that piece; the semantic_grid shows P for the selected piece and S for other selectables.
